name: Slack
description: Slack via Slack MCP server — messages, channels, search, user directory

messages.notes: Message history read — medium-risk due to potential sensitive content in channels

search.notes: Workspace-wide search — medium-risk, respects Slack's existing channel access controls

post.notes: Posting messages on behalf of the bot — write/high-risk, requires explicit approval and must be restricted to designated channels

users.notes: User directory and profile lookup — read-only

auth.notes: Slack Bot User OAuth Token (xoxb-...). Socket Mode app tokens (xapp-...) for event subscriptions if needed

notes: STATUS: Planned — evaluate @slack/mcp-server or official Slack MCP when available
Bot must be installed to the workspace by a Slack admin during onboarding
Bot only sees channels it has been invited to — scope naturally limits access
Message post capability (chat:write) is write/high-risk — restrict to approved channels only
Namespace prefix: all tools surfaced as slack.<tool_name>
Rate limits: Tier 3 methods allow ~50 requests/minute per workspace
